summary: Send update_highest_message.json `message` as a JSON integer, not a string.
reason: upsertInboxMessages originally returned the highest seen message ID only as a Go string. Passing that string into the POST body produced "message":"789", which Pushover's type-strict update_highest_message.json endpoint rejects — server-side cleanup silently failed even though the local SQLite store updated. The function now also returns the parsed int64, and the inbox-sync caller uses it (gated on highestNum >= 0) so non-numeric fallback IDs never reach the API.
validated_outcome: go test ./internal/cli -run TestUpsertInboxMessages -count=1 covers both the numeric-highest path and the all-hashed-IDs fallback (highestNum=-1) that suppresses the delete-through POST.
